title: cornus sanguinea subsp. hungarica
intro: Cornus sanguinea subsp. hungarica, also known as Hungarian dogwood, is a subspecies of the dogwood plant, characterized by its striking red berries and dark green leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Hungarian dogwood include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting potential antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
